channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bitcoin flirts with $100k as crypto industry rallies for Trump
item.description: Bitcoin has blasted through a series of record highs since President-elect Trump’s election victory as the crypto industry anticipates a significant shift in federal policy.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Bluesky Expands Moderation Team, Introduces Verification Measures Amid User Growth
item.description: Bluesky expands moderation team to combat verification and impersonation issues as the platform experiences rapid user growth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Forget the Fujifilm X100VI –why the leaked GFX100RF is the most exciting camera of 2025
item.description: New Fujifilm leaks suggest it's planning to launch a new GFX medium format compact camera –here's why that's an exciting prospect.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Revolutionizing Surgery: Robotic System Trained with AI and Imitation Learning
item.description: Johns Hopkins University researchers train robotic surgical system to perform complex tasks with human-like precision using artificial intelligence and imitation learning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: Thousands of Amazon workers around the world went on strike Friday, demanding more pay and better working conditions during one of the retail giant's busiest weekends of the year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Tech Ecosystem Powering Latin America’s Growth
item.description: Even for those of us optimistic about Latin America’s (LATAM) business prospects, the path ahead isn’t without challenges. Political tensions persist. Several major economies in the region continue to seek balance and stability.
item.dc:creator: Nacho De Marco, Fast Company

item.title: How I turned my blog into a social media hub
item.description: A few months ago, I decided to start blogging again. Even with steady freelance work and a couple of tech advice newsletters, I missed having my own little place on the web to write about any topic I pleased, free from the limits and vagaries of social media.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who had bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canada sues Google claiming ‘near-total control’ of online ads
item.description: Canada’s antitrust watchdog said Thursday it was suing Google over its alleged anticompetitive conduct in online advertisements.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Joshua trees are in peril. California has a plan to save them
item.description: The California Department of Fish and Wildlife has released a new plan to protect the state’s iconic Joshua trees, which are imperiled by wildfires, human development and climate change.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US police departments put EV patrol cars to the test
item.description: Some local police departments are considering moving to electric vehicles to bolster their fleet of patrol vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Kotlin Drops Scripting-Related Technologies: What It Means for Developers
item.description: JetBrains announces plans to remove scripting-related technologies from Kotlin language, affecting REPL functionality, Java scripting API, and other components.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CISA debuts new cybersecurity training platform
item.description: The new "CISA Learning" system will offer the same training to both internal staff and tens of thousands of external users. The post CISA debuts new cybersecurity training platform first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: GitHub Copilot: Everything you need to know
item.description: In 2014, Microsoft Research released an experimental Bing Code Search add-on for Visual Studio and the web. It was a code snippet search tool for C# with a natural language interface, using an index of code from Stack Overflow, MSDN, Dotnetperls, and CSharp411, powered by Bing, running on Azure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don’t get caught in the 'Apple ID suspended' phishing scam
item.description: Even the most tech-savvy computer users have to think twice with some of the clever phishing emails scammers are coming up with these days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
